title: cleome spinosa var. horrida
intro: Cleome spinosa var. horrida is a species of flowering plant in the genus Cleome, commonly known as the 'prickly spider flower'. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Cleome spinosa var. horrida include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. The herbal preparations of Cleome spinosa var. horrida typically involve using the plant's leaves and flowers, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
